disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden-onset, sudden-offset rapid palpitations lasting about 15 minutes, resolving abruptly and spontaneously ('like a switch flipped')
trigger_question_examples: Tell me exactly how it started and how it stopped.
Did it come on gradually or all at once?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It came on completely out of the blue while I was just sitting there, raced flat out for about 15 minutes, and then just switched off on its own.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The racing felt perfectly regular and steady, not chaotic or irregular
trigger_question_examples: When it was racing, did it feel regular and even, or irregular and all over the place?
Was the fast beat steady like a drum, or jumping around unevenly?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It was fast but really regular and even — a steady pounding, not fluttery or jumping around.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Presyncope during the episode — lightheaded and nearly fainted, had to lie flat on the floor
trigger_question_examples: Did you feel faint, dizzy or like you might pass out during it?
Did you have to sit or lie down?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, I got really lightheaded and felt like I was going to pass out, so I had to lie flat on the floor until it stopped.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Mild chest tightness/discomfort and shortness of breath accompanying the high-rate episode
trigger_question_examples: Did you have any chest pain, tightness or trouble breathing while it was racing?
Any chest discomfort or shortness of breath during the episode?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My chest felt tight and I was pretty short of breath while it was going, but that settled once it stopped.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Recurrent — three identical sudden episodes this year
trigger_question_examples: Has this happened before? How many times?
Is this the first time, or have you had episodes like this previously?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: This is the third time this year — they're all the same, sudden on and sudden off.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Young, otherwise completely healthy with excellent exercise tolerance and no known heart problems
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any medical conditions or heart problems?
How's your general health and fitness normally — can you exercise okay?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm really healthy and fit normally, no heart problems that I know of, and I can exercise fine.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: High stimulant intake — regular energy drinks and pre-workout supplements plus moderate caffeine
trigger_question_examples: Do you use caffeine, energy drinks, or pre-workout supplements?
How much coffee or how many energy drinks do you have in a day?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, I drink energy drinks and take pre-workout most days, and a fair bit of coffee too.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Strong anxiety about the unpredictability of the episodes (the plausible-but-wrong panic-attack anchor)
trigger_question_examples: How are you feeling about all this?
What's worrying you most about these episodes?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Honestly I'm scared — I never know when it will hit, and living alone makes it worse. Could it just be panic attacks?
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: Maybe I'm just an anxious person and it's all in my head?
